# WI-QUIC-CRT-0046 - QUIC server-role ALPN selection work item

## Summary

Preserve peer ClientHello ALPN offers, thread the configured local server protocol list into the managed TLS bridge, and emit exactly one selected ALPN protocol inside the existing server EncryptedExtensions path without widening the cryptographic floor.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0148

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0044

## Planned Changes

- Extend the managed server-role ClientHello handling to preserve enough ALPN offer information to select one configured local protocol honestly instead of emitting EncryptedExtensions without ALPN.
- Thread the existing validated listener or server-auth application-protocol list into the managed TLS bridge and key schedule before server-role handshake progression begins.
- Emit one ALPN extension carrying only the selected protocol inside the existing local EncryptedExtensions formatter, and fail deterministically with no_application_protocol when the peer omits ALPN, offers only unsupported protocols, or sends malformed or duplicate ALPN input.
- Add focused requirement-home tests for positive mutual-protocol selection, negative missing-ALPN and no-overlap failure, replay-style regression coverage for the preserved post-HRR quic-go failure lane, and a fuzz test over ALPN offer ordering, duplication, and malformed lengths.
- Keep the permanent benchmark coverage on the server handshake parser and continuation path current so the added offer preservation and selection logic remains measured.

## Out of Scope

- client-side ALPN verification against configured offers
- x25519 acceptance
- hybrid named-group acceptance
- general TLS group negotiation
- certificate-flight changes beyond the existing server seam
- 0-RTT
- 1-RTT
- key update
- interop-runner classification changes
- public API widening
- native TLS fallback

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for positive server-role ALPN selection, negative missing/no-overlap ALPN failure, and the replay-style post-HRR server handshake regression; rerun the preserved server-local quic-go handshake lane rooted at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-125529196-server-nginx/`; run fuzz coverage for ALPN offer ordering, duplication, and malformed lengths on the server ClientHello path; run the updated benchmark suite for the server handshake parser/continuation path; then render and validate SpecTrace artifacts.

## Completion Notes

Implemented the bounded server-role ALPN selection seam on the existing managed EncryptedExtensions path. The slice preserves the peer ClientHello ALPN offer, selects exactly one configured local protocol by local configuration order, fails missing, unsupported, duplicate, or malformed ALPN with no_application_protocol before ServerHello publication, and keeps the supported cryptographic floor unchanged.
